rulings.Case Title: JUAN CESPEDES et al VS. CENTERSTONE REALTY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Disqualify Mcdonnell From Representing Erickson And Or Woodstone In This Litigation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 19, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFFS JUAN CESPEDES, and, CARMEN CESPEDES Motion to Disqualify McDonnell from representing Erickson and or Woodstone in this Litigation. Off Calendar, case settled. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HIN LAND INVESTMENT LIMITED, A HONG KONG VS. GORDON UHLMANN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 19, 2010, line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT GORDON UHLMANN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion is granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to breach of contract cause of action as a matter of law based on the merger doctrine. Motion granted as to declaratory relief and injunction in Uhlmann's favor. Injunction to operate prospectively, i.e. Hin Land does not need to repaint the house. Parties to meet and confer regarding the terms of the injunction. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN BROWN et al VS. TROPHY PROPERTIES XIV DE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 19, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFFS SUSAN BROWN, DAVID DAVIS, JOSH CEAZAN, and, JOSE ESCOBAR Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Motion granted in part, and denied in part. Plaintiff's extrinsic evidence stricken and ruling based on review of defendants' answer only. Motion granted with 30 days leave to amend as to Trophy Properties XV, DE, LLC's 7th, 8th, 9th, 10th, 11th, 12th, 13th, 14th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 23rd affirmative defenses. As to other answer, motion granted with 30 days leave to amend as to 2nd (defendant to allege code section(s)) and 4th affirmative defenses. Motion denied in all other respects. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: INTERFUSION TECHNOLOGY, LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED VS. ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY, A BUSINESS ENTITY et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 19, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE, INC. Motion for Leave to Amend Cross-Complaint. Motion granted. The motion is timely and no prejudice to Interfusion demonstrated. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET BARROWS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 19, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT ADDUS HEALTHCARE INC. AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part, and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to first cause of action (Elder Financial Abuse) and third cause of action (unfair business practices). As to first cause of action, plaintiff must plead facts showing Addus took or assisted in the taking, or knew about the conduct by others. As to third cause of action, plaintiff must specify a particular statutory scheme that has been violated by Addus that supports this claim. Demurrer overruled as to second cause of action (Elder Abuse). =(302/CWW)
